patterns.name: Back-rank mate
patterns.description: Mate the king trapped on its back rank behind its own pawns.

patterns.name: Smothered mate
patterns.description: A knight mates a king hemmed in by its own pieces.

patterns.name: Deflection
patterns.description: Force a defender away from a key square or piece.

patterns.name: Discovered attack
patterns.description: Moving one piece unveils an attack from another.

patterns.name: Double check
patterns.description: Two pieces give check at once; the king must move.

patterns.name: Fork
patterns.description: One piece attacks two or more targets at once.

patterns.name: Hanging piece
patterns.description: Win an undefended or under-defended piece.

patterns.name: Pin
patterns.description: A piece cannot move without exposing a more valuable one behind it.

patterns.name: Sacrifice
patterns.description: Give up material for a decisive attack or gain.

patterns.name: Skewer
patterns.description: A valuable piece is forced to move, exposing a lesser one behind it.
